displayTitle: Two Probes Are Better Than One

beats.narration: One crystal can't talk and listen at once. While it rings, anything just under the surface hides. The fix: split the job.

payload.title: SPLIT THE JOB
payload.sub: Separate send from receive, and the dead zone disappears.

beats.narration: A dual probe splits the job: transmit and receive crystals flank a cork barrier, tilted so their beams cross, and near-surface reflectors reappear.
Pitch-catch: one sends, one receives. Tandem hunts vertical flaws singles miss. The flaw deflects the beam to the back wall; the trailing probe catches it.
Through-transmission: fixture both probes on one axis, set a reference on good material, read any drop as a shadow, never a depth.

chips.t: Fixture both probes on one axis
Set a reference on good material
Read a drop as a shadow

beats.narration: Here's the rule: match the arrangement to the flaw plane. If a single probe already sees the flaw, keep it simple.

payload.sub: If a single probe sees it, keep it simple.
